heading: Authorization of appropriations; reports to Congressional committees
label: 459j–8 Authorization of appropriations; reports to Congressional committees
notes: Amendments1994—Subsec. (a). , in introductory provisions substituted “Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate and the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives” for “Committees on Interior and Insular Affairs of the United States Congress”.Pub. L. 103–437, § 6(m)(1)Subsec. (b). , struck out at end “Within three years from , the Secretary shall develop and transmit to the Committees on Interior and Insular Affairs of the United States Congress a final master plan for the full development of the seashore consistent with the preservation objectives of sections 459j to 459j–8 of this title, indicating:Pub. L. 103–437, § 6(m)(2)January 3, 1975“(1) the facilities needed to accommodate the health, safety, and recreation needs of the visiting public;“(2) the location and estimated cost of all facilities; and“(3) the projected need for any additional facilities within the seashore.”1988—Subsec. (b). substituted “$2.6 million in addition to the sums previously appropriated” for “not more than $500,000”.Pub. L. 100–564
amendments: Amendments1994—Subsec. children.heading: Acquisition of lands and interests in lands
children.label: a Acquisition of lands and interests in lands

children.text: the amount of land acquired during the current fiscal year and the amount expended therefor;
the amount of land remaining to be acquired; and
the amount of land programed for acquisition in the ensuing fiscal year and the estimated cost thereof.

children.heading: Development of essential public facilities
children.label: b Development of essential public facilities
children.text: For the development of essential public facilities there are authorized to be appropriated $2.6 million in addition to the sums previously appropriated.
